validation_scope.cross_section_semantics: validated against declared FS-6000/8000/10000/11000 contracts and local references
validation_scope.cross_section_identifier_resolution: deferred to FS-14000 integration gate because canonical external packages were not included in this input

cross_section_permission_and_classificat.detail: All six local roles preserve human approval authority; service_agent is draft-only and cannot approve, export, or delete. External identifier-level resolution requires the canonical FS-6000 package at integration release.

Role capabilities cover tenant user, tenant admin, security admin, platform operator, auditor, and service agent visibility/operation classes. Workspace route-level resolution remains an FS-10000 integration-gate check.
service_agent is purpose-, workflow-, and tool-limited and has no independent approval/export/delete authority. Agent/tool ID resolution remains an integration-gate check.

Controls explicitly preserve public/private/synthetic/attested/inferred states, field/evidence classification, retention, export, and tenant boundaries. Exact policy-ID resolution remains an integration-gate check.

cross_section_permission_and_classificat.target: objects, fields, search, graph, documents, evidence, agent context, exports
cross_section_permission_and_classificat.detail: Explicit release-candidate test matrix covers every required surface.

cross_section_permission_and_classificat.target: provider approval, inventory, data eligibility, instruction versioning, evaluation, suspension
cross_section_permission_and_classificat.detail: Explicit mandatory control set covers every required model-governance control.

cross_section_permission_and_classificat.target: owner, trigger, steps, escalation, evidence, closure
cross_section_permission_and_classificat.detail: Each required playbook now contains an explicit release-candidate contract with all six elements.

release_notes: Tenant-isolation coverage explicitly includes objects, fields, search, graph, documents, evidence, agent context, and exports.
Model governance explicitly includes provider approval, model inventory, data eligibility, prompt/instruction versioning, evaluation, and suspension.
Incident, BCP/DR, vendor-risk, and change-management playbooks explicitly include owner, trigger, steps, escalation, evidence, and closure.
Integrity files use a non-recursive checksum policy: PACKAGE_INDEX.json inventories INTEGRITY_INDEX.csv; neither file attempts a self-checksum.
